req.title: Manager creates benefit
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a benefit through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
preconditions: authenticated
role:manager
benefit module is enabled

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) authenticated manager can POST to /api/benefits and receive 200/201 with entity id; (2) mandatory fields must be validated (400 on missing); (3) unauthenticated requests yield 401; (4) wrong-role requests yield 403; (5) an audit record is written on success. Tests are designed using EP (valid partition), NEG (missing fields, wrong role, no auth), BVA (name at max boundary length), DT (role × auth decision table), and STC (resource lifecycle: absent → created). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create/clean isolated state.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_benefit_successfully
preconditions: Benefit module is enabled
Caller is authenticated as a user with role=manager
Payload contains all mandatory fields with valid values
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token> fixture
POST /api/benefits with a complete, valid payload
Assert HTTP status is 200 or 201
Assert response body contains a non-null entity id
Query the audit log via <get_audit_record> helper and assert record exists
expected: Response JSON contains 'id' field with a non-null value
An audit record is written for the created benefit
trace: R-WEB-033: authenticated manager can create a benefit
R-WEB-033: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-033: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_creates_benefit_successfully(manager_token, api_client, get_audit_record, delete_benefit):
    payload = {
        "name": "Health Insurance",
        "type": "HEALTH",
        "description": "Full medical coverage",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01"
    }
    response = api_client.post(
        "/api/benefits",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    benefit_id = body.get("id")
    assert benefit_id is not None, "Response must contain entity id"

    audit = get_audit_record(entity="benefit", entity_id=benefit_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist after benefit creation"
    assert audit.get("action") == "CREATE"

    delete_benefit(benefit_id, manager_token)

tests.name: test_create_benefit_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: Benefit module is enabled
Caller is authenticated as a user with role=manager
Payload omits the mandatory 'name' field
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token> fixture
POST /api/benefits with payload missing the 'name' field
Assert HTTP status is 400
Assert response body contains a validation error referencing 'name'
expected: Response body describes a validation error for the missing field
trace: R-WEB-033: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_create_benefit_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: Benefit module is enabled
No Authorization header is sent (unauthenticated caller)
actions: POST /api/benefits with a fully valid payload but without any auth token
Assert HTTP status is 401

tests.name: test_create_benefit_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: Benefit module is enabled
Caller is authenticated with role=employee (not manager)
actions: Obtain a valid employee auth token via <get_employee_token> fixture
POST /api/benefits with a fully valid payload
Assert HTTP status is 403

tests.name: test_create_benefit_name_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Benefit module is enabled
Caller is authenticated as a user with role=manager
API defines max name length as 255 characters
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token> fixture
Construct a benefit name that is exactly 255 characters long
POST /api/benefits with the boundary-length name and all other mandatory fields
Assert HTTP status is 200 or 201
Assert response body contains a non-null entity id
Clean up by deleting the created benefit
expected: Benefit with a 255-character name is created successfully
Response JSON contains 'id' field with a non-null value
trace: R-WEB-033: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-033: response 200 or 201 with entity id
BVA: name field at upper boundary of allowed length
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 255

def test_create_benefit_name_at_max_length_boundary(manager_token, api_client, delete_benefit):
    boundary_name = "B" * MAX_NAME_LENGTH
    assert len(boundary_name) == MAX_NAME_LENGTH

    payload = {
        "name": boundary_name,
        "type": "HEALTH",
        "description": "Boundary length name test",
        "effective_date": "2024-06-01"
    }
    response = api_client.post(
        "/api/benefits",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    benefit_id = body.get("id")
    assert benefit_id is not None, "Response must contain entity id for boundary-length name"

    delete_benefit(benefit_id, manager_token)

tests.name: test_create_benefit_name_exceeds_max_length_returns_400
preconditions: Benefit module is enabled
Caller is authenticated as a user with role=manager
API defines max name length as 255 characters
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token> fixture
Construct a benefit name that is 256 characters long (one over the boundary)
POST /api/benefits with the over-limit name and all other mandatory fields
Assert HTTP status is 400
Assert response body contains a validation error referencing 'name'
expected: Response body describes a validation error for the 'name' field exceeding max length
trace: R-WEB-033: mandatory fields must be validated
BVA: name field one character above upper boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 255

def test_create_benefit_name_exceeds_max_length_returns_400(manager_token, api_client):
    over_limit_name = "B" * (MAX_NAME_LENGTH + 1)
    assert len(over_limit_name) == MAX_NAME_LENGTH + 1

    payload = {
        "name": over_limit_name,
        "type": "HEALTH",
        "description": "Over-limit name test",
        "effective_date": "2024-06-01"
    }
    response = api_client.post(
        "/api/benefits",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    errors = body.get("errors") or body.get("detail") or ""
    assert "name" in str(errors).lower(), "Validation error must reference 'name' field length"

tests.name: test_benefit_state_transition_absent_to_created
preconditions: Benefit module is enabled
Caller is authenticated as a user with role=manager
The benefit to be created does not currently exist (absent state)
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token> fixture
Confirm no benefit with the test name exists via <find_benefit_by_name> helper
POST /api/benefits with a uniquely-named valid payload
Assert HTTP status is 200 or 201
Re-query the API to confirm resource transitions to 'created' state (GET /api/benefits/{id})
Clean up by deleting the created benefit
expected: Benefit transitions from absent state to created state
HTTP 200 or 201 is returned on creation
GET /api/benefits/{id} returns the benefit with correct data after creation
trace: R-WEB-033: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_benefit_state_transition_absent_to_created(manager_token, api_client, delete_benefit):
    unique_name = f"STC-Benefit-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    auth_header = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}

    payload = {
        "name": unique_name,
        "type": "LIFE",
        "description": "State transition test benefit",
        "effective_date": "2024-03-01"
    }
    create_resp = api_client.post("/api/benefits", json=payload, headers=auth_header)
    assert create_resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Create failed: {create_resp.status_code}"

    benefit_id = create_resp.json().get("id")
    assert benefit_id is not None

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/benefits/{benefit_id}", headers=auth_header)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Created benefit must be retrievable"
    assert get_resp.json().get("name") == unique_name

    delete_benefit(benefit_id, manager_token)
